output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 5/5.
Batch 1: Verified batch 1 tasks `T1` and `T3` in the current workspace state and checkpointed the results. `ClipAction.tsx` already contains the pinned-shot-group menu gating/no-op behavior, and `useClipResize.ts` already carries primary+adjacent resizes through one shared transaction with one merged `applyEdit`. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. Focused video-editor vitest hit only the known pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` TimelineCanvas failure; per instruction, I also ran the full `npx vitest run`, which still fails broadly on unrelated pre-existing workspace issues (27 failed files / 54 failed tests total).
Batch 2: Verified batch 2 tasks `T2`, `T4`, and `T6` in the current workspace state and checkpointed the results. `TimelineEditor` passes `isInPinnedShotGroup` into each `ClipAction`, the widened resize callback types flow through the timeline state/provider/editor surfaces, and `ClipAction.test.tsx` covers the pinned-shot-group context-menu gating cases. Verification passed with `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`.
Batch 3: Verified batch 3 task `T5` and checkpointed the result. `TimelineCanvas.tsx` already had the pinned-shot-group adjacent-resize wiring in place; this pass confirmed the neighbor resolution, dual resize overrides, grouped-edge snap bypass, primary-drag min-duration clamp, and single `onActionResizeEnd` callback shape, and removed stray shot-group debug logging. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` still fails only on the known pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` assertion, and the required full `npx vitest run` still fails broadly on unrelated pre-existing workspace issues (27 failed files / 54 failed tests total).
Batch 4: Verified batch 4 task `T7` and checkpointed the result. `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` already contained the requested pinned-shot-group resize coverage: the interior left-resize case asserts one start/end callback with adjacent populated, the free outer-edge case asserts adjacent undefined, and the min-duration case asserts the primary drag is clamped. Running `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` produced 10 passing tests for those behaviors and only the known pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` failure. Per the batch rule, `npx vitest run` was rerun and continued surfacing the same broad unrelated pre-existing workspace failures seen in prior batches.
Batch 5: Executed the batch-5 verification stack. `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` confirmed `ClipAction.test.tsx` passes and the targeted pinned-shot-group resize cases in `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` pass; the only focused failure remains the known pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` assertion. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. Per the batch rule, `npx vitest run` was rerun and continued surfacing the same broad unrelated pre-existing workspace failures seen in earlier batches, so the full suite is still not green for reasons outside this scoped video-editor work. A throwaway verifier script was created under `/tmp`, run successfully, and deleted; it re-ran the focused modules, asserted the targeted resize/menu behaviors, and printed the manual in-product smoke steps. The manual browser smoke itself, including the single-undo confirmation, was not executed in this CLI-only environment.
deviations: No new code edits were required in this turn because the batch-1 implementations were already present in the shared workspace; this pass verified them and checkpointed status.
Focused video-editor vitest and the full suite still fail on pre-existing unrelated workspace issues, including the known `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` TimelineCanvas assertion.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the existing ClipAction implementation: it threads `isInPinnedShotGroup`, gates pinned-group menus down to asset-state items only, removes the trailing divider when nothing follows, and no-ops right-click and stale-badge opens when no asset-state action is available. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed; focused vitest only failed on the already-known pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` TimelineCanvas assertion.

Verified the existing useClipResize implementation: resize start records primary and adjacent origins under one shared transaction id, resize end computes primary then adjacent updates in memory, merges rows/meta updates, commits with exactly one `applyEdit`, and clears both ref entries. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed; `npx vitest run` still reports broad pre-existing unrelated failures plus the known TimelineCanvas pinned-group assertion.

Verified the existing TimelineEditor wiring: `getActionRender` passes `isInPinnedShotGroup={shotGroupClipIds.has(action.id)}` into `ClipAction`, and `shotGroupClipIds` remains in the memo dependency list. Verification passed with `npx tsc --noEmit` and the focused video-editor vitest run.

Verified the existing type widening across `useTimelineState.types.ts`, `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`, `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, and `TimelineEditor.tsx`: the optional `adjacent` payload is represented through named resize callback types and compiles cleanly without changing existing call sites. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and the focused video-editor vitest run also passed.

Verified the existing ClipAction regression coverage for pinned-shot-group no-op context menus, pinned-shot-group asset-only menus, and stale-badge no-op behavior, while keeping the existing non-grouped cases intact. Running the full `ClipAction.test.tsx` file as part of the focused video-editor module run passed.

Verified the existing TimelineCanvas auto-shift wiring: pointer-down resolves the adjacent clip from the pinned shot group, drag writes resize overrides for both clips, snap-to-sibling is skipped only for interior grouped edges, primary drag clamps to preserve adjacent min duration, and resize end emits one callback with the adjacent payload while clearing both overrides. Removed stray shot-group debug logging from TimelineCanvas during this pass. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` still fails only on the known pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` assertion, and the required full `npx vitest run` still fails broadly on unrelated pre-existing workspace issues (27 failed files / 54 failed tests total).

Verified that `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` already contains the requested pinned-shot-group resize coverage: the interior left-resize case asserts one start/end callback with adjacent populated, the free outer-edge case asserts adjacent remains undefined, and the min-duration case asserts the primary drag is clamped while preserving one callback each. Running `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` produced 10 passing tests for those behaviors and only the known pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` failure. Per the batch rule, a full `npx vitest run` was rerun against the current workspace state and continued to report the same broad unrelated pre-existing suite failures seen in prior batches.

Ran the requested focused verification stack: `ClipAction.test.tsx` passed, `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` kept the three requested pinned-shot-group resize cases green, and the only focused failure remained the known pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` assertion. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. Per the batch rule, `npx vitest run` was rerun against the current workspace state and continued surfacing the same broad unrelated pre-existing failures seen in earlier batches, so the full suite is still not green for reasons outside this video-editor scope. A throwaway verifier script was created under `/tmp`, executed successfully, and deleted; it re-ran the focused ClipAction/TimelineCanvas modules, asserted the targeted resize/menu behaviors, and printed the manual in-product smoke steps. The manual browser smoke itself, including single-undo confirmation, was not executed in this CLI-only environment.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: ClipAction threads `isInPinnedShotGroup`, hides split/mute/shot/delete sections inside pinned groups, keeps only asset-state items, suppresses the trailing divider when those hidden sections disappear, and no-ops both right-click and stale-badge clicks when no asset action can render.

useClipResize records both primary and adjacent origins at resize start under one shared transaction id, computes each clip update through the shared helper, applies primary then adjacent in memory, and commits the combined rows/meta updates through exactly one `applyEdit` before clearing both ref entries.
TimelineEditor passes `isInPinnedShotGroup={shotGroupClipIds.has(action.id)}` into each rendered `ClipAction`, and `shotGroupClipIds` remains in the `getActionRender` dependency list.

The widened resize callback types are named in `useTimelineState.types.ts` and explicitly threaded through `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`, `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, and `TimelineEditor.tsx`; `npx tsc --noEmit` passed without call-site breaks.

ClipAction tests cover the pinned-shot-group no-op right-click, the pinned-shot-group stale-reminder menu with only asset actions visible, and the pinned-shot-group stale-badge no-op; the focused video-editor test run passed with the existing non-grouped assertions still green.

TimelineCanvas resolves the grouped neighbor at resize pointer-down, carries both clip overrides through drag, skips `snapResize` only when an interior grouped neighbor is present, clamps the primary edge to protect the adjacent clip's minimum duration, and calls `onActionResizeEnd` exactly once with the adjacent payload before clearing both overrides.

The TimelineCanvas module already covers all three requested resize cases: interior grouped left drag with adjacent populated and one start/end callback each, free outer-edge drag with adjacent undefined, and neighbor min-duration protection via a clamped primary start asserted from `onActionResizeEnd`.

Focused video-editor verification landed in the expected state: `ClipAction.test.tsx` passed, the targeted `TimelineCanvas` resize tests passed, and only the known pinned-data-attribute failure remained in that module. The full suite still does not pass because of broad unrelated pre-existing workspace failures. The throwaway verifier script confirmed the automated gating and adjacent-follow checks and printed the manual undo smoke steps, but an in-browser single-undo smoke run was not performed from this environment.
